html:
Holcim Elevate Membranes: What Changed From Firestone

Owners who had a roof installed a decade ago under the Firestone Building Products name sometimes get confused when a current bid references Holcim Elevate instead. It is the same manufacturer's roofing division under new ownership and a new name, not a different company, and the product lines an existing roof was built with are generally still supported under the new branding.

The Ownership Change, Plainly Stated

Firestone Building Products, the commercial roofing manufacturing division, was acquired by Holcim, a global building materials company based in Switzerland, and the roofing business was rebranded as Elevate, later folded into the broader Holcim Elevate name. The manufacturing facilities, product formulations, and much of the technical support structure carried forward through that transition, so an existing Firestone-installed roof is not suddenly an orphaned system without support.

For owners with an older roof under the original branding, this matters mainly at claim or warranty-transfer time, when confirming who currently administers the warranty and how to reach that group is worth doing directly rather than assuming the paperwork from a decade ago still points to the right contact.

We run into this most often on buildings that changed hands since the original roof went in, where the new owner has a warranty document with an unfamiliar company name on it and no clear idea whether it still means anything. Tracking down current administration on that paperwork before assuming it is worthless saves owners from either ignoring real coverage or, just as often, assuming coverage exists when it has actually lapsed for unrelated reasons.

Current Product Line for Odessa Applications

Holcim Elevate's commercial roofing catalog includes TPO membrane in standard and fleece-backed configurations, EPDM in both black and white options, and a range of insulation, coating, and accessory products designed to work within the same system. That breadth covers most of what comes up on Odessa reroofs, from a straightforward TPO recover on a warehouse to an EPDM patch-and-repair job on an older building already carrying that membrane.

The company's coating product line is also relevant to the restoration side of this market, where an aging metal roof or single-ply membrane is a candidate for a liquid-applied coating rather than a full tear-off, and having coating products engineered to work with the company's existing membrane lines simplifies that recommendation.

This matters on the fabrication shops and warehouse buildings around Odessa where an owner is not ready for a full reroof but needs to stop active weathering on a metal roof or aging single-ply membrane before it turns into an interior leak.

  • TPO membrane in standard and fleece-backed thicknesses
  • Black and white EPDM membrane and compatible accessories
  • Restoration coating products for aging metal or membrane roofs
  • Insulation and cover board products within the same system family
  • Legacy support for roofs originally installed under the Firestone brand

Continuity of Warranty Coverage After the Rebrand

A common owner question is whether a warranty issued years ago under the Firestone name is still honored now that the division operates as Holcim Elevate. In general, corporate rebrands of this kind carry forward existing warranty obligations under the successor entity, since the underlying business and its contractual commitments continued rather than dissolving. That said, we recommend confirming the specific warranty document and current contact information directly with the manufacturer for any older roof, rather than assuming continuity without verifying it.

For new installations going forward, warranty terms and contractor program requirements are set under the current Holcim Elevate structure, and those terms should be reviewed at the time of contract rather than compared against how the program worked years ago under the previous name.

We keep a copy of current manufacturer contact and claims-submission information on file rather than relying on whatever number was printed on an original warranty card, since administrative contacts and submission processes shift over time even when coverage itself remains intact.

What This Means for a New Roof Specification

For a new project, whether a roof carries the Holcim Elevate name or a competing manufacturer's brand matters less than whether the specific product, thickness, and attachment method fit the building's exposure to hail, wind, and traffic. We evaluate Holcim Elevate's current TPO and EPDM options against other manufacturers' comparable products on those merits for each project rather than defaulting to brand familiarity from past jobs.

Questions on Holcim Elevate Roofing

Is my old Firestone roof still covered by warranty now that the company is Holcim Elevate?

Generally yes, since the underlying business and its obligations carried forward through the rebrand, though we recommend confirming the specific warranty terms and current contact directly for any older roof.

Does Holcim Elevate still make EPDM as well as TPO?

Yes, the current product line includes both EPDM in black and white and a full TPO lineup in standard and fleece-backed options.

Will parts and accessories from my old Firestone roof still be available for repairs?

Many legacy products remain supported, though availability can vary by specific product and age of the original installation. We check current availability before quoting a repair.

Is Holcim Elevate a reliable choice for a new commercial roof in this market?

It is a viable option among several major manufacturers, and we evaluate its specific products against the building's needs the same way we would evaluate any other manufacturer's system.

Who do I contact if I have questions about an old Firestone-branded warranty?

Current manufacturer contact information is available through Holcim Elevate directly, and we can help identify the right point of contact for an existing warranty inquiry.
